name: Original Toll House Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a small bowl, combine flour, baking soda, and salt; set aside.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat butter until creamy. Gradually add granulated sugar and brown sugar, beating well after each addition. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each egg is incorporated. Blend in vanilla extract.
4. Gradually beat in the flour mixture. Stir in chocolate morsels and nuts.
5. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough onto ungreased cookie sheets.
6. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until edges are golden brown. Cool completely on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
7. Makes about 5 dozen cookies.
TOLL HOUSE PAN COOKIES:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Prepare dough as directed and spread it evenly in a greased 15-1/2" x 10-1/2" baking pan.
3. Bake for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown. Cool completely on the pan before slicing into 2-inch squares.
4. Makes about 3 dozen cookies.
REFRIGERATOR TOLL HOUSE COOKIES:
1. Prepare dough as directed, then divide it in half and wrap each portion separately in waxed paper.
2. Refrigerate for 1 hour or until firm.
3. On waxed paper, shape each dough half into a log measuring approximately 15 inches long. Wrap the logs again in waxed paper and refrigerate for an additional 30 minutes.
4. The dough may be stored up to one week in the refrigerator or up to eight weeks in the freezer if wrapped in foil or freezer-safe plastic wrap.
5. To bake, preheat oven to 375°F. Cut each log into 1/2-inch slices and place them on ungreased cookie sheets. Bake for 8-10 minutes until edges are golden brown.
6. Makes about 5 dozen cookies.
